title: Services is not accessible outside of the host.
body:
/kind bug

**Description**

I have tried with my samba service and docker.io/magicloud/httpd image. All images and confs worked well with Docker. With the same CLI parameters but `podman`, the containers were created correctly, programs ran correctly, ports were listened correctly (in containers and host). But the services were only accessible from with the host (host itself, another container, a NAT VM), but not other hosts in the same subnet.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. `sudo podman run -d -p80:80 docker.io/magicloud/httpd`

**Describe the results you received:**

On another host, connection to this service returned timeout. On podman host, it worked well.

**Describe the results you expected:**

Accessing the service from other hosts.

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:      2.0.4
API Version:  1
Go Version:   go1.14.6
Built:        Thu Jan  1 08:00:01 1970
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

** CNI **

```
$ cat /etc/cni/net.d/87-podman-bridge.conflist 
{
  "cniVersion": "0.4.0",
  "name": "podman",
  "plugins": [
    {
      "type": "bridge",
      "bridge": "cni-podman0",
      "isGateway": true,
      "ipMasq": true,
      "hairpinMode": true,
      "ipam": {
        "type": "host-local",
        "routes": [{ "dst": "0.0.0.0/0" }],
        "ranges": [
          [
            {
              "subnet": "10.88.0.0/16",
              "gateway": "10.88.0.1"
            }
          ]
        ]
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "portmap",
      "capabilities": {
        "portMappings": true
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "firewall"
    },
    {
      "type": "tuning"
    }
  ]
}
```

** iptables **

```
$ sudo iptables -L -t nat
Chain PREROUTING (policy ACCEPT)
target     prot opt source               destination         
DOCKER     all  --  anywhere             anywhere             ADDRTYPE match dst-type LOCAL
CNI-HOSTPORT-DNAT  all  --  anywhere             anywhere             ADDRTYPE match dst-type LOCAL

CNI-HOSTPORT-DNAT  all  --  anywhere             anywhere             ADDRTYPE match dst-type LOCAL

Chain POSTROUTING (policy ACCEPT)
target     prot opt source               destination

MASQUERADE  all  --  172.17.0.0/16        anywhere

MASQUERADE  tcp  --  172.17.0.3           172.17.0.3           tcp dpt:ssh
CNI-ce2678d981ebc124fdec43f9  all  --  10.88.0.13           anywhere             /* name: "podman" id: "37a1fa688438d4314fe4f28235536385c4139807c81264d5b283dd21608ca2de" */

Chain CNI-DN-ce2678d981ebc124fdec4 (1 references)
target     prot opt source               destination         
CNI-HOSTPORT-SETMARK  tcp  --  10.88.0.0/16         anywhere             tcp dpt:http
CNI-HOSTPORT-SETMARK  tcp  --  localhost            anywhere             tcp dpt:http
DNAT       tcp  --  anywhere             anywhere             tcp dpt:http to:10.88.0.13:80

Chain CNI-HOSTPORT-DNAT (2 references)
target     prot opt source               destination         
CNI-DN-ce2678d981ebc124fdec4  tcp  --  anywhere             anywhere             /* dnat name: "podman" id: "37a1fa688438d4314fe4f28235536385c4139807c81264d5b283dd21608ca2de" */ multiport dports http

Chain CNI-HOSTPORT-MASQ (1 references)
target     prot opt source               destination         
MASQUERADE  all  --  anywhere             anywhere             mark match 0x2000/0x2000

Chain CNI-HOSTPORT-SETMARK (2 references)
target     prot opt source               destination         
MARK       all  --  anywhere             anywhere             /* CNI portfwd masquerade mark */ MARK or 0x2000

Chain CNI-ce2678d981ebc124fdec43f9 (1 references)
target     prot opt source               destination         
ACCEPT     all  --  anywhere             10.88.0.0/16         /* name: "podman" id: "37a1fa688438d4314fe4f28235536385c4139807c81264d5b283dd21608ca2de" */
MASQUERADE  all  --  anywhere            !base-address.mcast.net/4  /* name: "podman" id: "37a1fa688438d4314fe4f28235536385c4139807c81264d5b283dd21608ca2de" */

Chain DOCKER (2 references)
target     prot opt source               destination         
RETURN     all  --  anywhere             anywhere            
RETURN     all  --  anywhere             anywhere            
RETURN     all  --  anywhere             anywhere            
RETURN     all  --  anywhere             anywhere            
DNAT       tcp  --  anywhere             anywhere             tcp dpt:filenet-tms to:172.18.0.2:2080
DNAT       tcp  --  anywhere             anywhere             tcp dpt:filenet-rpc to:172.18.0.3:8123
DNAT       tcp  --  anywhere             anywhere             tcp dpt:ms-wbt-server to:172.17.0.3:3389
DNAT       tcp  --  anywhere             anywhere             tcp dpt:ssh to:172.17.0.3:22
```

comments.body: Podman deliberately does not create firewall rules to allow traffic to ports - you'll need to add a rule to allow traffic on port 80 to the host. Podman will forward it to the container from there.
@mheon Is there a way to let Podman to do it?
